title: Exact spectrum and anomalous relaxation in the open disorder-free Sachdev-Ye-Kitaev system
authors: Soshun Ozaki, Hironobu Yoshida, Hosho Katsura
abstract: We study a disorder-free variant of the Sachdev-Ye-Kitaev (SYK) model with dissipation within the Gorini-Kossakowski-Sudarshan-Lindblad formalism. By utilizing the integrability of the clean SYK model, we derive an exact solution in a spectrum-resolved form, i.e., the eigenvalues and corresponding projection superoperators of the Liouvillian for arbitrary system size $N$. We determine the scaling of the gap that governs the long-time decay of the two-point correlation functions. Importantly, the gap does not vanish in the dissipationless limit when the thermodynamic limit is taken first, despite the integrability of the model. This phenomenon, known as anomalous relaxation, suggests a possible connection with chaotic dynamics and quantum Ruelle-Pollicott resonances. We also find several spectral features, such as transitions in the Liouvillian spectrum from complex to real eigenvalues with increasing dissipation strength, as well as the convergence of the dissipative form factor to the spectral form factor in the dissipationless limit. These findings indicate that the present model offers a useful platform for exploring nontrivial open dynamics of many-body quantum systems.
summary: This paper analyzes the open Sachdev-Ye-Kitaev (SYK) model by solving its master equation exactly using techniques derived from its integrable clean limit. The key finding is 'anomalous relaxation,' where a non-zero gap persists in the thermodynamic limit even when dissipation vanishes. This suggests deep connections between quantum chaos and steady-state dynamics in many-body systems.
main_problem: The study investigates quantum chaos and anomalous relaxation phenomena in open, interacting many-body systems.
main_result: The system exhibits anomalous relaxation because the gap governing long-time decay does not vanish in the dissipationless limit when the thermodynamic limit is taken first. This suggests a connection to chaotic dynamics.
method: The authors derive exact solutions for the Liouvillian spectrum and time evolution by utilizing the integrability of the clean SYK model within the GKSL formalism.
model_or_system: The system is a disorder-free variant of the Sachdev-Ye-Kitaev (SYK) model subjected to dissipation, governed by a Lindblad master equation. The Hamiltonian involves four-body couplings, and dissipation is introduced via jump operators.
key_observables: Liouvillian spectrum ($\lambda_m$), projection superoperators ($\Pi_m$), the Dissipative Form Factor (DFF), and the steady-state two-point correlation function $	ext{C}_{i,j}^\infty(t)$.
important_parameters: Dissipation strength ($\Gamma$), system size ($N$), and the limit taken ($\lim_{N	o\infty}\lim_{\Gamma	o 0}$ vs. $\lim_{\Gamma	o 0}\lim_{N	o\infty}$).
main_assumptions: The analysis relies heavily on the integrability of the clean SYK model to derive exact spectral solutions.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the transition of Liouvillian eigenvalues from complex to real as dissipation increases, and compare the DFF across various $\Gamma$ values against theoretical scaling forms (SFF).
paper_structure: The paper proceeds by establishing the formal solution for the density matrix using a CONS basis, deriving explicit expressions for the Liouvillian eigenvalues, calculating the time evolution of correlation functions via the adjoint superoperator, and finally analyzing the limits to pinpoint anomalous relaxation.
why_interesting: This work provides an exact theoretical framework for studying non-equilibrium dynamics in strongly interacting quantum systems, particularly highlighting how dissipation interacts with underlying integrability structures.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
